meta.name: PAS Panic and Agoraphobia Scale
meta.description: The PAS is a 13-item self-report scale designed to assess the severity of panic disorder with or without agoraphobia. It covers five domains: panic attacks, agoraphobic avoidance, anticipatory anxiety, disability, and health concerns.
meta.author: Bandelow B (1995)
meta.reference: Bandelow B. Assessing the efficacy of treatments for panic disorder and agoraphobia. II. The Panic and Agoraphobia Scale. Int Clin Psychopharmacol. 1995;10(2):73-81.
meta.instruction: Please rate how much the following problems have bothered you during the past week.

questions.text: How many panic attacks did you have in the past week?
How severe were your panic attacks (considering intensity of symptoms)?
How long did your panic attacks last on average?
Did you have fear or worry about having another panic attack?
Did you avoid certain places or situations because of fear of having a panic attack?
Did you avoid being alone because of fear of having a panic attack?
Did you avoid crowded places (e.g., shops, public transport, cinemas)?
Did you avoid traveling long distances from home?
Were you restricted in your daily activities because of your panic or anxiety?
Were you restricted in your social life or leisure activities?
Were you restricted in your work or household duties?
Were you worried about your health because of the physical symptoms during panic?
Were you worried that you might have a serious illness (e.g., heart disease)?

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score indicates no significant panic or agoraphobia symptoms. You are functioning well in this area.
suggestions: Practice regular relaxation and stress management

ranges.label: Mild Panic/Agoraphobia
ranges.description: You are experiencing mild symptoms of panic or agoraphobia. These symptoms may cause some discomfort but are not significantly impairing.
suggestions: Learn about panic disorder and its symptoms
Practice breathing exercises and grounding techniques
Gradually expose yourself to feared situations with support
Consider consulting a mental health professional if symptoms persist

ranges.label: Moderate Panic/Agoraphobia
ranges.description: You are experiencing moderate panic or agoraphobia symptoms that are likely affecting your daily functioning.
suggestions: Seek evaluation from a mental health professional
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for panic disorder
Avoid caffeine and other stimulants that may worsen symptoms

ranges.label: Severe Panic/Agoraphobia
ranges.description: Your symptoms are severe and likely causing significant impairment in multiple areas of life.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional treatment promptly
Consider a combination of medication and psychotherapy
Reach out to your support network for help
Do not attempt to face feared situations alone without professional guidance

ranges.label: Very Severe Panic/Agoraphobia
ranges.description: Your symptoms are very severe. Panic attacks and avoidance behavior are likely dominating your daily life. Immediate professional help is strongly recommended.
suggestions: Please contact a mental health professional or visit a hospital immediately
Medication may be necessary to reduce acute symptoms
Ensure you have a safety plan and support person available
Remember that panic disorder is highly treatable with proper care
